title: Euodia orbicularis
taxonomy: Euodia orbicularis is classified in the family Rutaceae. It is a species of flowering plant within the genus Euodia. The Rutaceae family is also known as the citrus or rue family, and it includes a wide variety of plants such as citrus and orange trees. Euodia orbicularis is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Australia. This species is a shrub or small tree with fragrant flowers.
uses: Euodia orbicularis is traditionally used in traditional medicine, particularly in China. It is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is thought to be useful in treating conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Additionally, Euodia orbicularis may also be used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and sore throats. More research is needed to fully confirm the medicinal uses and effectiveness of Euodia orbicularis.

parts: Euodia orbicularis, also known as Chinese rowan or Chinese rowanberry, is a plant native to China. Its medicinal parts, including the root, bark, and leaves, have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for various purposes. The root of Euodia orbicularis is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for fever and infections. side_effects: Euodia orbicularis, also known as the Chinese box or Chinese boxwood, is a plant native to Asia. However, there is limited information on its side effects in humans.
